diagnosis.finding: split-half template reliability is NOT a coherent reliability for this apparatus: the unweighted template average is norm-dominated by the bare {w} template, so any half CONTAINING it reproduces the full geometry and any half WITHOUT it measures a different object.
diagnosis.consequence: reliability<validity paradox resolved as apparatus artifact; disattenuation undefined; v1 gates cannot be evaluated. Deeper: the validated template-averaging convention (anchors npz, Atlas v0, anatomy v0) measures a bare-word-dominated geometry - interpretation, not values, affected. v2 (norm-equalized averaging) is the corrective test.
